goal: 让 cxs 默认保留已索引历史而不是暴露 Codex 归档 root

summary.title: 默认保留已索引历史
summary.what: Changed cxs sync so missing raw source files are no longer treated as deletions by default. Added an explicit sync --prune option for callers that intentionally want to delete indexed rows that disappeared from the current source snapshot. Updated README, architecture/coverage docs, and the distributable cxs skill so agents query the SQLite index normally instead of switching to Codex-specific archive roots.
summary.why: The user wanted Codex raw-log archival to be invisible to CXS users and agents. CXS should treat Codex JSONL files as ingest input, while the SQLite index remains the query surface for already indexed history.
summary.user_goal: 让 cxs 默认保留已索引历史而不是暴露 Codex 归档 root

decisions.point: Default strict sync behavior when a previously indexed source file is absent from the current source snapshot
decisions.chose: Retain the indexed session row and still write coverage for the current source snapshot.
decisions.rationale: This preserves historical query/read behavior after raw log maintenance and keeps Codex storage layout out of the cxs user workflow.
rejected: Keep pruning missing source files during every successful strict sync.
Teach users or skills to query a separate Codex archive root.

decisions.point: How to support intentional cleanup of rows missing from source
decisions.chose: Add explicit sync --prune to run the old source-snapshot reconciliation deletion path.
decisions.rationale: Deletion remains available, but it is now an opt-in cleanup action rather than the normal incremental sync behavior.

decisions.point: How to document Codex raw-log movement
decisions.chose: Describe raw JSONL as an ingest source and avoid archive-root examples in the skill package.
decisions.rationale: The agent-facing workflow should stay archive-agnostic and continue using find/list/read against the cxs index.

rejected.alternative: Expose ~/.codex/archived_sessions as another root in the normal cxs workflow.
rejected.reason: That leaks a Codex implementation detail into CXS usage and contradicts the user requirement for archive-agnostic querying.

review_notes: Verification: npm run check passed with 25 files and 131 tests.
Verification: npm run build passed and prepared dist/cli.js.
Smoke: global cxs sync --cwd /Users/envvar/work/repos/browser-brain-loop reported sourceFileCount 0, indexedSessionCount 510, removed 0.
Smoke: global cxs find E_VERIFY_FAILED --cwd /Users/envvar/work/repos/browser-brain-loop --sort ended returned an indexed March 2026 session.
Smoke: archive/archived_sessions/归档 strings are absent from repo docs, skill package, and installed global cxs skill.

architectural_claims: Codex JSONL files are an ingest source, not the query truth once content is indexed.
CXS queries and reads should remain archive-agnostic and operate against the SQLite index.
behavioral_changes: sync no longer deletes indexed sessions solely because their source file is absent from the current source snapshot.
sync --prune explicitly deletes indexed sessions whose source file is absent from the selected source snapshot.
coverage can report a current source file count lower than the retained indexed session count.

data_model_changes.name: coverage indexed_session_count semantics
data_model_changes.migration_notes: No schema migration. The count may include retained indexed sessions whose raw files are absent from the current source snapshot.

migration_notes: No database schema migration is required.
Existing installed local cxs CLI and global cxs skill were refreshed outside the commit for this machine.
